summary:
The short, sharp pain from a tooth being felt in the brain is primarily carried through A-delta nerve fibers that conduct fast, localized nociceptive impulses. This explains how dental pain can be perceived centrally despite originating in the oral cavity.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{A}}$

Only include this summary; do not add extra text or explanation.
